description_long: This helicopter features a sleek, angular fuselage with a predominantly black exterior and a streamlined design. The cockpit is fitted with large, sharply angled windows that have a blue tint, providing wide visibility for the pilots. The main rotor consists of two long, straight black blades mounted on a central mast, while the tail section extends back to a small, angular tail rotor. The helicopter is supported by two robust, black landing skids with crossbars for stability. The overall appearance is modern and tactical, with no visible registration markings or decorative elements. The surface finish is smooth and slightly glossy, suggesting a metal or composite construction.
description: A modern black helicopter with blue-tinted cockpit windows, two main rotor blades, and sturdy landing skids. The design is angular and streamlined, with a smooth, slightly glossy finish.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the helicopter, blue-tinted cockpit windows, main rotor, and landing skids.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the angular cockpit windows, the width of the landing skids, and the central rotor mast.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the tail section, cockpit windows, and landing skids from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the tail boom, tail rotor, and the alignment of the landing skids.
attribute_rotor_blades: Two long, straight main rotor blades, black in color, with a simple, unmarked design. The blades appear to be made of composite or metal material and extend well beyond the width of the fuselage.
attribute_tail_rotor: A small, two-bladed tail rotor is present at the end of the tail boom, oriented vertically. The blades are black and appear to be unmarked, with no visible protective shroud.
attribute_landing_skids: The helicopter has two parallel, black landing skids with crossbars for structural support. The skids are straight and robust, likely made of metal or composite material.
attribute_cockpit_windows: There are four large, sharply angled cockpit windows arranged in a wraparound configuration. The windows are blue-tinted, trapezoidal in shape, and framed in black.
